topic: Execution Agent — AI-Powered Order Execution Layer
summary: Implemented execution_agent.py as the 7th Claude agent in the Decifer pipeline. The agent sits between the 6-agent signal pipeline and IBKR order placement, reasoning about market microstructure (spread width, relative volume, VWAP position, time-of-day, conviction score, and regime) to decide HOW to execute each pre-approved trade. Replaces the static rule-based fill_watcher config with per-trade dynamic parameters.
files_changed: orders.py (add _get_ibkr_bid_ask, execution agent call, order-type branch, watcher_params passthrough)
key_decisions: Lazy import of execution_agent inside execute_buy() avoids circular imports and keeps orders.py load fast
Separate _get_ibkr_bid_ask() helper (does NOT modify _get_ibkr_price) preserves all existing test mocks
fallback_on_error=True ensures a failed Claude call never blocks a trade — always falls through to static CONFIG values
System prompt is fixed/cacheable (cache_control: ephemeral) — variable market data in user message only
FillWatcher.watcher_params defaults to None for backward compatibility — all existing tests pass unchanged
zoneinfo (stdlib since 3.9) used for ET timezone — no new dependency

test_results.combined_run_note: 7 test_tranche_exits failures in fill_watcher→tranche→orders_core ordering are PREEXISTING (confirmed present in original code with same ordering) — caused by test module identity mismatch from dual eviction of orders.py in test_tranche_exits.py and test_orders_core.py module-level setup. Not introduced by this PR.

next_steps: Paper-trade run: confirm execution_agent_plan events appear in audit_log.jsonl with non-fallback reasoning
Monitor: check fill quality on wide-spread vs tight-spread trades after 1 week of paper trading
Consider adding audit event logging for execution_agent plan (currently only logged via INFO)
